description: A React Native (Expo) starter wired for harness-engineering workflows. Features are the scope primitive — agents work on exactly one in_progress feature at a time.

features.title: Harness scaffolding present and verified
features.description: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, init.sh, feature_list.json, claude-progress.md, and docs/ exist. init.sh exits 0 on a fresh clone.
verification: Run ./init.sh from a fresh clone; it exits 0
Run npm run verify; it exits 0
AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/HARNESS.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md are all present
feature_list.json validates against scripts/feature-list-check.js
features.notes: Closed 2026-05-19. ./init.sh ran end-to-end on a fresh checkout (1052 packages installed, ~1m). npm run verify all four steps green at SHA d04fc81: tsc clean, expo lint clean, 6/6 jest tests pass, feature_list.json schema valid.

features.title: Home screen shows project name and harness status
features.description: The default tab renders the project name, the count of done vs total features from feature_list.json, and the last claude-progress.md headline. This proves the JS bundle, file-system access pattern, and routing are all wired up.
verification: Start the app: npm run start
Open on iOS sim, Android emulator, or web
Verify the home tab shows the project name 'rn-harness'
Verify the home tab shows feature counts that match feature_list.json
Snapshot test covers the rendered counts
features.notes: Read the file at build/bundle time, not runtime — RN's file system is not Node's.

features.title: Feature list screen — view-only list of all features and their status
features.description: A second tab renders every feature from feature_list.json with its id, title, status, and a coloured status pill. Read-only; editing the list is out of scope for this feature.
verification: Tab is reachable from the bottom tab bar
All features in feature_list.json appear
Status pill colour matches: todo=grey, in_progress=blue, blocked=red, done=green
RTL layout renders correctly (toggle device language to Arabic in the sim)
Component is covered by a render test
features.notes: Demonstrates list virtualisation discipline — use FlashList or FlatList, never .map over a long array of Views.

features.title: Maestro smoke flow runs on a local simulator
features.description: Scaffold the Maestro toolchain into the harness so feature verification lists can include real-simulator runs. The smoke flow exercises the default Expo home tab end-to-end (bundle loads, scaffold text visible, tab navigation works) and drops artifacts in tmp/diagnostics/.
verification: Install maestro: curl -Ls 'https://get.maestro.mobile.dev' | bash
Boot an iOS simulator: npm run sim:ios
Start the app on the sim: npm run ios (or open in Expo Go)
Run the flow: npm run e2e (or npm run e2e:ios)
Confirm exit code is 0
Confirm a screenshot artifact appears under tmp/diagnostics/ named ...-maestro-home-*.png
Confirm the JUnit report appears under tmp/diagnostics/ named ...-maestro-home.junit.xml
features.notes: Scaffolding (config.yaml, flows/home.yaml, run-maestro.sh, sim-ios.sh, sim-android.sh, npm scripts) landed on branch feat/e2e-001-maestro. The verification list is intentionally simulator-bound and was not exercised in the scaffolding session — an agent with a Mac + Xcode picks this up to flip the feature to done.
